# Security Checklist: Kafka Component and Dashboard

**Purpose**: Validate whether the feature requirements clearly and completely specify Keycloak-backed OAuth/SSO expectations for all in-scope operator-facing platforms and documented exceptions.
**Created**: 2026-03-24
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/mehdi/MyProject/elegant-infra-engine/specs/001-add-kafka-dashboard/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Do the requirements explicitly identify every in-scope operator-facing platform or interface that must use Keycloak-backed OAuth/SSO when technically supported, rather than naming only the Kafka dashboard? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the spec define what "technically supported" means for a platform so exception decisions are not left to interpretation? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Gap, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK003 Are platform-specific exception requirements documented for cases where a component cannot support Keycloak OAuth/SSO, including required rationale and fallback access model? [Completeness, Gap, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK004 Are authentication requirements defined separately from general external-access requirements so public reachability does not implicitly mean unauthenticated access? [Completeness, Conflict, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-011, Spec §Assumptions]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is the statement that authentication will "follow the platform defaults" replaced or supported by explicit Keycloak SSO requirements for this feature? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK006 Are the required identity flow characteristics specified clearly enough to distinguish OAuth/OIDC login, session handling, and logout expectations from generic "SSO" wording? [Clarity, Gap, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK007 Does the spec define which user roles or operator personas must be able to authenticate through Keycloak and whether any anonymous access is permitted? [Clarity, Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK008 Are terms such as "public platform entry point" and "external access" clarified so they cannot be misread as public unauthenticated exposure? [Clarity, Ambiguity, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-011]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Are the authentication expectations consistent between the external-access requirements and the assumption that operational interfaces follow platform defaults? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-011, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK010 Do standalone component requirements and aggregate-stack requirements impose the same Keycloak SSO expectations and exception handling rules? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-003, Spec §User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK011 Are the dashboard access requirements consistent with the success criteria, or do the measurable outcomes omit authentication prerequisites that would materially affect access time and operator workflow? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Spec §SC-002]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK012 Are acceptance criteria defined for successful Keycloak-backed sign-in on each supported operator-facing platform in scope? [Acceptance Criteria, Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK013 Are the exception acceptance criteria measurable for unsupported platforms, including how the spec determines that Keycloak OAuth/SSO is unavailable and what approved alternative is allowed? [Measurability, Gap, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK014 Can compliance with the authentication requirement be objectively verified from the written spec without relying on implied platform conventions? [Measurability, Ambiguity, Spec §Assumptions]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK015 Are requirements defined for first-time operator access through Keycloak as well as repeat access when an SSO session already exists? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK016 Are failure-path requirements specified for Keycloak unavailable, unreachable, or misconfigured scenarios, distinct from Kafka or cluster dependency failures? [Coverage, Exception Flow, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK017 Are recovery requirements defined for restoring operator access after a Keycloak-side outage or configuration mismatch without weakening the intended authentication policy? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK018 Are Keycloak, OAuth/OIDC, and any platform-default identity dependencies explicitly documented as feature dependencies rather than left as implicit environmental assumptions? [Dependency, Gap, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK019 Does the spec document the assumption that all relevant platforms can integrate with Keycloak, and require evidence or a decision record when a platform cannot? [Assumption, Gap, Spec §Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK020 Does the spec resolve the ambiguity between "open source dashboard" selection and authentication requirements, making clear whether Keycloak compatibility is a mandatory selection criterion? [Ambiguity, Gap, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Assumptions]

## Notes

- Depth: Standard
- Audience: Reviewer (PR)
- Focus: Keycloak-backed OAuth/SSO requirement quality across all in-scope operator-facing platforms and documented exception handling
